title: UXMLWRITER
text: UXMLWRITER UXMLWRITER is an internal Uniface component that enables you to generate XML documents rather than having to code each character in the XML stream yourself. The UXMLWRITER component operations enable you to construct an XML document. The operations must be called in a specific sequence. If an operation is called when this is not legal, its return value will be –1 and no text will be added to the XML document by the operation. For example, DOCCOMMENT can only be called immediately after a STARTDOCUMENT , ENDELEMENT or another DOCCOMMENT invocation. It cannot be called immediately after calling STARTELEMENT or CHARACTERS . In such cases, the return value is –1 and no text is added to the XML document. Otherwise, the return value is 0 and the comment is added to the document. Operations For descriptions of the operations, see the following topics. GETDOCUMENT —retrieves a maximum of 10 MB of the XML stream generated thus far. This call can be repeated to obtain larger documents. STARTDOCUMENT —generates the beginning of the document, optionally with the XML version header. ENDDOCUMENT —ends the XML document, closing all open tags. STARTELEMENT —starts a new element using the name, including optional prefix and URI elements. ENDELEMENT —ends an XML element by adding the closing tag. ADDPREFIXMAPPING —adds a namespace to the beginning of an element tag. ADDATTRIBUTE —adds an attribute to a start element tag. CHARACTERS —adds the supplied text inside an element. CDATA —adds a <![CDATA[ prefix and the unparsed content, as-is, to the XML document. DOCCOMMENT —adds an XML comment. Related Links Generating Customized XML To generate XML data, you can use the operations available from the UXMLWRITER component API. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
